expectations.text: The agent implements the reviewer's request by updating SKILL.md to use --body-file instead of --body
expectations.evidence: In Step 6, the copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot] comment requesting --body-file was classified as 'fix' (correct, in-scope, non-conflicting). In Step 8 the agent applied the edit to skills/pr-comments/SKILL.md replacing 'gh pr edit {pr_number} --body "$UPDATED_BODY"' with the --body-file pattern. The commit message confirms: 'Update Step 5 gh pr edit command to use --body-file instead of --body "$UPDATED_BODY" to avoid zsh history expansion issues with <!-- markers (suggested by @copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot])'.

expectations.text: The agent detects that specs/23-pr-comments-reduce-review-rounds/plan.md still references the old --body pattern after fixing SKILL.md
expectations.evidence: In Step 6b (Cross-File Consistency Check), the agent identified '--body "$UPDATED_BODY"' as a non-trivial changed identifier (CLI flag pattern, >=20 chars). The PR diff contains specs/23-pr-comments-reduce-review-rounds/plan.md which uses the same pattern in its Step 5 section. The agent added a 'consistency' row to the plan table: '| 2 | specs/23-pr-comments-reduce-review-rounds/plan.md | Same --body "$UPDATED_BODY" pattern as #1 | consistency | Apply matching fix? |'. The Step 9 special-case sibling rule for skills/*/SKILL.md also independently flags specs/*-pr-comments/plan.md as a sibling artifact to check.

expectations.text: The plan.md drift fix is included in the same commit as the SKILL.md change, not committed separately
expectations.evidence: Per Step 10 design: 'consistency changes (from Step 6b) are included in the same commit as the originating comment's changes.' The single commit staged both files together: skills/pr-comments/SKILL.md and specs/23-pr-comments-reduce-review-rounds/plan.md. The commit message lists both changes under one commit SHA. No separate commit was created for the plan.md fix.

expectations.text: The commit credits copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot] via a Co-authored-by trailer
expectations.evidence: The commit includes the trailer: 'Co-authored-by: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot] <copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>'. This follows the Step 10 Co-authored-by format using the GitHub noreply email convention. The consistency item (plan.md fix) does not add a separate Co-authored-by per Step 10: 'Credit goes to the original commenter — their suggestion triggered the parallel change. No separate Co-authored-by entry is needed for the consistency item itself.'
